tools.name: scrape

timeout.description: Maximum time to wait for page load in milliseconds. Increase for slow-loading sites (e.g., 120000 for 2 minutes). Default: 60000 (1 minute)

maxChars.description: Maximum number of characters to return from the scraped content. Useful for limiting response size. Default: 100000

startIndex.description: Character position to start reading from. Use with maxChars for pagination through large documents (e.g., startIndex: 100000 to skip first 100k chars). Default: 0

resultHandling.description: How to handle scraped content and MCP Resources. Options: "saveOnly" (saves as linked resource, no content returned), "saveAndReturn" (saves as embedded resource and returns content - default), "returnOnly" (returns content without saving). Default: "saveAndReturn"

forceRescrape.description: Force a fresh scrape even if cached content exists for this URL. Useful when you know the content has changed. Default: false

cleanScrape.description: Whether to clean the scraped content by converting HTML to semantic Markdown of what's on the page, removing ads, navigation, and boilerplate. This typically reduces content size by 50-90% while preserving main content. Only disable this for debugging or when you need the exact raw HTML structure. Default: true

findings.title: Excessive description length in 'scrape'
findings.description: Description is 1827 chars (limit 1000). May contain hidden content.
findings.remediation: Keep descriptions concise; review for hidden instructions.

findings.title: SSRF (timing) — 'scrape.url' delayed on SSRF payload
findings.description: Call with payload 'http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/' took 10.6s vs baseline 2.3s — server likely attempted the outbound request before timing out.
findings.remediation: Block outbound requests to RFC-1918 and link-local ranges. Use an allowlist for permitted URL schemes and hosts.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: Null request ID accepted — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server accepted a request with id=null and returned a result. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, null IDs are reserved for notifications (no response expected). Accepting them may cause response routing bugs in complex orchestration scenarios.
findings.remediation: Reject requests with null id values; treat them as notifications.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 1 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.
